REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (601 O'Farrell Street, #2002, San Francisco, Ca 94109)
Off Calendar Oct-07-2025 Written Order Received
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Ryan Meckfessel for trustee Debra Dolch, who was also present.
Jeff de Rhodes, buyer.
Jasmine Louie, agent.
Kevin Birmingham, broker.

The matter was called for hearing on Report of Sale and Petition to Confirm Sal of Real Property located at 631 O'Farrell Street, #2002, San Francisco, CA 94109.

The Court called for any overbidders. No overbids were received.

The sale was confirmed at $388,000 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to review of the order). An additional bond in the amount of $345,000 is required.

Judge: Cindee Mayfield; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Petition For Authority To Sell Trust Beneficiary?S Personal Residence And For Authority To Enter Into Exclusive Right To Sell Agreement
Off Calendar Jun-18-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Ryan Meckfessel for and with Debra Dolch, Trustee (via Zoom).
Christopher Jew, Court Appointed Attorney for Linda T. Keegan, Conservatee (via Zoom).

Mr. Jew reported that he met with Ms. Keegan last week and that she objects to the proposed sale of her condominium. She believes she may regain the ability to walk and return home, despite current medical assessments. The Court noted that her position appears contrary to the medical evaluations in the record.

Ms. Dolch and Mr. Meckfessel addressed the Court regarding Ms. Keegan's current health, stating she requires daily assistance and suffers from significant contractures. She was recently placed in an assisted living facility, and the physicians do not believe she will regain the ability to live independently.

Counsel noted that the trust holds two real properties. One will be rented; the other (the subject of the petition) is a vacant condominium in the Tenderloin. Due to high HOA fees and restrictions on rentals, sale of the property is sought in the best interest of the trust to fund Ms. Keegan's long-term care. Her Medi-Cal and Medicare coverage are now active, and her personal items have been secured following cleanup of the property.

Following discussion, the Court took the petition under submission with the intent to grant.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)
